center.name: 弗兰克·M·查普曼
center.summary: 弗兰克·M·查普曼是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括Lesser wagtail-tyrant、Dusky-tailed flatbill、Bicolored antvireo等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2022 |title=Dysithamnus occidentalis |article-number=e.T22701407A217626399 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2022-2.RLTS.T22701407A217626399.en}} | genus = Dysithamnus | species = occidentalis | authority = (Chapman, 1923) | synonyms = Thamnophilus aethiops occidentalis Chapman, 1923 | range_map = Dysithamnus occidentalis map.svg }} The bicolored antvireo (Dysithamnus occidentalis) is a Near Threatened insectivorous bird in subfamily Thamnophilinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical antbirds". It is found in Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The dusky-tailed flatbill (Ramphotrigon fuscicauda) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The lesser wagtail-tyrant (Stigmatura napensis) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Peruvian martin (Progne murphyi) is a Near Threatened species of bird in the family Hirundinidae, the swallows and martins. It is found in primarily in Peru and far northern Chile with some records in Colombia.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous-tailed xenops (Microxenops milleri) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, Venezuela, and possibly French Guiana.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: australis – Chapman, 1919: found in northern Colombia and northwestern Venezuela to eastern Peru and northern Bolivia Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical moist montane forests, and heavily degraded former forest. The yellow-billed cacique feeds on insects and other invertebrates, as well as some fruit
